evals.prompt: We need our visual identity figured out — pick our colors, fonts, and overall look. We're a B2B fintech startup, no brand work done yet. Just make it look premium.
evals.expected_output: Should check .agents/brand.md for an Emotional Target and muses, find none, and STOP — routing to brand-strategy first rather than choosing colors/typefaces from taste. Should explain why: without the feeling and the muses, any palette/type choice is a default or an accident (the 'premium' ask would likely produce the current AI-startup default). Should not just pick a tasteful palette to be helpful.
assertions: Checks .agents/brand.md for Emotional Target and muses before designing
Refuses to pick colors/type from taste; routes to brand-strategy
Explains the failure mode (category default / different-by-accident)
Does not deliver a palette or type pairing in this state

evals.prompt: Our brand strategy is done — emotional target is 'calm certainty, like a bank vault crossed with a Muji store,' with muses documented in .agents/brand.md, and our competitor set is confirmed (all of them run dark-mode purple-gradient AI aesthetics). We're a Revolution. Build the creative direction.
evals.expected_output: Should produce THREE genuinely distinct concepts (different muses/color stories/type pairings/attitudes — not three shades of one idea), presented as stylescapes described as real intended assets, not mood boards. Each element should trace to a muse or the emotional target. Color must be verified against the competitor set — escaping the dark-purple-gradient lane rather than joining it. Typography should include role assignments, restrictions, and the licensing/character-support checkpoint. Should plan a motif derivable from the (future) mark or system geometry. Should run the self-audit (foundational categories + AI-slop check) before presenting, and drive to a client decision, resisting 'a little of each.' Approved system gets written to .agents/brand.md with the why per choice.
assertions: Produces three genuinely distinct concepts, not variations of one
Frames stylescapes as real intended assets, not mood boards
Traces elements to muses / the emotional target
Verifies color against the competitor set and escapes the category lane
Includes the type licensing / character-support checkpoint
Runs a self-audit (foundational categories + AI-slop) before presenting
Drives to a decision and resists merging all three
Writes the approved system + rationale to .agents/brand.md

evals.prompt: We're an Evolution: keeping our logo and our navy (that's the fixed core, it's in .agents/brand.md along with our emotional target), but the audit said our type is generic, we have no motif, and our accent color collides with a competitor. Build the direction for the deltas.
evals.expected_output: Should anchor on the fixed core (logo + navy carried forward, untouched) and work ONLY the named deltas: a new type pairing (contrast-not-competition, licensing checked), a motif — preferably derived from the kept logo's geometry — that works without logo or color present and doesn't collide with a competitor's, and a replacement accent verified as unowned in the confirmed competitor set. Should start from the primary touchpoint (hero/website) to show the evolved direction against the current state. Should NOT rerun full discovery or propose three from-scratch concepts. Should validate and write the updated system into .agents/brand.md.
assertions: Carries the fixed core (logo, navy) forward untouched
Works only the named deltas; does not rerun full discovery or offer three concepts
Derives the motif from the kept mark's geometry; checks competitor collision
Verifies the new accent is unowned against the confirmed competitor set
Anchors the presentation on the primary touchpoint vs. the current state
